data.narrative:
Saronic is a Series D-stage defense and security company founded by Dino Mavrookas, Rob Lehman, Vibhav Altekar, and Doug Lambert, currently valued at $9.25B post-money on $2.6B raised to date. The comparable universe consists of ten defense and security firms across public and private markets: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers including Chaos Industries, Epirus, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Flock Safety, representing the same sector and adjacent funding stages.

Two valuation methods apply to Saronic. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yields $13.9B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables—Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), Skydio ($2.2B Series E), Epirus ($1.4B Series D), and Hidden Level ($500M Series C)—produces a median of $2.2B, with a range of $500M to $7.5B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B (low) to $16.0B (high), with a median of $8.0B. The current $9.25B valuation sits above the median, consistent with stage-progression logic but substantially above peer-median indication. Key uncertainties include the early-stage bias in the peer set—no Series D comparable exactly matches Saronic's maturity—and limited public market reference points in this sector.
